entries.definition:
-ي ʾ-l-w/y shortcoming, to be remiss; oath, to swear; to shine; favour. Favour, however, could also, on the basis of meaning and structure, be connected with the root أ/ل/ل ʾ-l-l (q.v.). Of this root,
41
إلى

four forms occur 37 times in the Qurʾan: يَأْلُونَ yaʾlūn once; يُؤْلُونَ yuʾlūn once; يَأْتَلِى yaʾtalī once and آلاء ʾālāʾ 34 times.

يَأْلُونَ yaʾlūn [imperf. of v. أَلَا ʾalā, trans./intrans.; this verb usually takes a negative particle and together they function as a single unit] to be reticent towards someone, fall short of doing what should be done; to flag; to be remiss (3:118) لا يَأْلُونَكُمْ خَبَالًا do not take for your intimates such outsiders as are unflagging in their efforts to ruin you.

يُؤْلُونَ yuʾlūn [imperf. of v. IV (آلى ʾaʾlā) orthographically > أَلَا ʾalā intrans.] to swear, to vow *(2:226) يُؤْلُونَ مِنْ نِسَائِهِمْ they swear/vow never to have sexual relations with their wives.

يَأْتَلِى yaʾtalī [imperf. of v. VIII ائْتَلَى ʾiʾtalā, intrans.] to swear (24:22) وَلا يَأْتَلِ أُولُو الْفَضْلِ مِنْكُمْ وَالسَّعَةِ أَنْ يُؤْتُوا أُولِى الْقُرْبَى وَالْمَسَاكِينَ وَالْمُهَاجِرِينَ فِى سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ and let not those of you who possess bounty and plenty forswear giving to kinsmen, the poor, and those who emigrate in God’s cause.

آلاء ʾālāʾ [pl. of n. [[unclear: ʾalan / ʾilan / ʾilyun]]] favour, blessing (53:55) فَبِأَىِّ آلَاءِ رَبِّكَ تَتَمَارَى so which of your Lord’s blessings do you doubt?

إلى ʾilā preposition (حرف جر) occurring 737 times in the Qurʾan. In context إلى mainly denotes: 1 a point reached in physical location (انتهاء الغاية المكانية) to, as far as (17:1) سُبْحَانَ الَّذِى أَسْرَى بِعَبْدِهِ لَيْلًا مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ الْأَقْصَى glory be to Him who made His servant travel by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Furthest Mosque 2 a point reached in time (انتهاء الغاية الزمانية) to, till, until (2:187) ثُمَّ أَتِمُّوا الصِّيَامَ إِلَى اللَّيْلِ then complete the fast until nightfall. Grammarians differ as to whether a point reached (انتهاء الغاية) means only the initial point or the entire extent of the location; consequently jurisprudents differ as to whether or not elbows should be included in the ablution as commanded in verse (5:6) يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ إِلَى الْمَرَافِقِ you who believe, when you are about to pray, wash your faces and your hands up to [also interpreted as: up to and including] the elbows 3 accompaniment ‘with’ (2:14) وَإِذَا خَلَوْا إِلَى شَيَاطِينِهِمْ when they

42
إلياس

are alone with their evil companions 4 the meaning of preposition fī (الظرفية أو معنى في) in; at; on; near, by … (4:87) لَيَجْمَعَنَّكُمْ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ He will gather you all together on the Day of Resurrection. It is also said to have the meaning of فِى in (79:18) هَلْ لَكَ إِلَى أَنْ تَزَكَّى do you want to purify yourself? 5 direction towards (19:25) وَهُزِّى إِلَيْكِ بِجِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ تُسَاقِطْ عَلَيْكِ رُطَبًا جَنِيًّا and shake the trunk of the palm tree towards you, it will drop over you ripe dates 6 the meaning of preposition لِ (معنى حرف الجر ل) ‘to’ (27:33) وَالْأَمْرُ إِلَيْكِ فَانْظُرِى مَاذَا تَأْمُرِينَ the command belongs to you, so consider what orders to give us 7 the meaning of preposition ʿalā (معنى على) against; upon (in one interpretation of 17:4) وَقَضَيْنَا إِلَى بَنِى إِسْرَائِيلَ فِى الْكِتَابِ لَتُفْسِدُنَّ فِى الْأَرْضِ مَرَّتَيْنِ وَلَتَعْلُنَّ عُلُوًّا كَبِيرًا in the Scripture, We decreed upon the Children of Israel, ‘Twice you will spread corruption in the land and become highly arrogant’ 8 in addition to (38:24) لَقَدْ ظَلَمَكَ بِسُؤَالِ نَعْجَتِكَ إِلَى نِعَاجِهِ he has wronged you by demanding your ewe [to add] to his ewes 9 so-called ‘redundant’ (زائدة), imparting emphasis to the entire context, as in the variant reading of تَهْوِى tahwī as تَهْوَى tahwā in (14:37) فَاجْعَلْ أَفْئِدَةً مِنَ النَّاسِ تَهْوِى (تَهْوَى) إِلَيْهِمْ so make [some] hearts of humankind turn to them, (or, to yearn for them) 10 connecting an agent to an object (التعدية) (12:33) قَالَ رَبِّ السِّجْنُ أَحَبُّ إِلَىَّ مِمَّا يَدْعُونَنِى إِلَيْهِ he said ‘My Lord! the prison is more preferable to me than what they are asking me to do.’ Grammarians note, however, that a goal (الغاية), of some kind or another, is always present as a semantic component in all contexts in which إلى occurs.

إلياس ʾilyās [proper name of Hebrew origin occurring three times in the Qurʾan] the prophet Elijah (I Kings XVII.ff. and II Kings I–II) (6:85) وَزَكَرِيَّا وَيَحْيَى وَعِيسَى وَإِلْيَاسَ كُلٌّ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ Zachariah, John, Jesus and Elijah—every one of them was righteous; the story is told of how he commanded his people to refrain from the worship of Baal (37:123–32). It is in the context of this narrative that a quaint etymological rendering of the term ʾilyāsīn occurs *(37:130) سَلَامٌ عَلَى إِلْ يَاسِينَ peace be upon [the family of] ʾil yāsīn interpreted as either إلياس ʾilyās himself or a plural of [[unclear: إلياس]], meaning him and his followers. It is possible, although there is no transmitted authority, that إل ʾil could mean آل ʾāl, ‘the people of’,

43

are alone with their evil companions 4 the meaning of preposition fī (الظرفية أو معنى في) in; at; on; near, by … (4:87) لَيَجْمَعَنَّكُمْ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ He will gather you all together on the Day of Resurrection.
